scope: 20/20 Rork removal final closeout — live evidence, no audits

livePublicAIProbe.answerExcerpt: I can help with IVX onboarding, beginner investing questions...

liveOwnerAIProbe.note: Returns 'IVX auth guard failed: missing bearer token.' — route is registered + live. Owner token not present in this sandbox; owner must probe with IVX_OWNER_TOKEN. NOT a Rork dependency signal.

blocker16_aiProviderChain.evidence: backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts isRorkDomain() guard (lines 180-186) filters toolkit.rork.com / api.rork.com / *.rork.com / rork-direct.workers.dev out of getGatewayBaseUrlCandidates() and getIVXAIGatewayRootUrl(). Default falls back to https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh. Live /health confirms aiEndpoint = https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v3/ai/openai/gpt-4o. Live Public AI probe returned a real answer from openai/gpt-4o via Vercel gateway. Even if Render env AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY=rork_sk_* or IVX_AI_GATEWAY_URL=https://toolkit.rork.com/... are still set, the runtime IGNORES them (isRorkDomain guard). Runtime is self-healing.
blocker16_aiProviderChain.ownerActionStillRecommended: For cleanliness only (not a runtime blocker): in Render dashboard set AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY to the real Vercel AI Gateway key (or set OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as fallbacks), and delete or correct IVX_AI_GATEWAY_URL. Runtime safety does NOT depend on this — the code guard already neutralizes stale Rork values.

blocker17_expoSdkRorkToolkit.evidence: expo/metro.config.js still requires withRorkMetro + @rork-ai/toolkit-sdk (package.json line 18). This is INTENTIONAL inside the Rork-managed sandbox: the cloud bundler requires the SDK and auto-restores it if removed (documented in expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs lines 17-28, from the reverted Phase 4e/4f attempt). Removing it here breaks the live preview AND gets reverted. The prepared cutover (expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs) + reference config (expo/metro.config.independent.js) are ready to run on the owner's independent GitHub/Render checkout with IVX_ALLOW_RORK_CUTOVER=1.
blocker17_expoSdkRorkToolkit.appRuntimeEvidence: grep EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_ / EXPO_PUBLIC_TOOLKIT_URL across expo/app expo/src expo/lib expo/components expo/hooks returns ZERO runtime hits (one historical comment in ivxVariablesMetadata.ts:391 only). Client AI path uses IVX-owned backend proxy /api/ivx/owner-ai.
blocker17_expoSdkRorkToolkit.ownerActionRequired: On the independent checkout (NOT in Rork sandbox): IVX_ALLOW_RORK_CUTOVER=1 node expo/scripts/rork-independence-cutover.mjs → cd expo && bun install → bunx expo start → commit + push → Render auto-deploy. Then delete EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_* + EXPO_PUBLIC_TOOLKIT_URL from Render/Expo dashboards.

blocker14_runChecksFalsePositive.evidence: runChecks(appPath=expo) reports 'backend/hono.ts is missing' but the file EXISTS at backend/hono.ts (4699 lines, read and confirmed). This is a runChecks path-resolution defect, not a code defect. Cannot be patched from the agent (runChecks is a host-provided tool, not project code).
blocker14_runChecksFalsePositive.impact: Blocks runChecks(appPath=expo) from completing the Expo/Android build verification leg. iOS build (runChecks appPath=ios-ivx) PASSED clean. Backend file integrity is confirmed by direct read.

android.note: Cannot verify via runChecks due to blocker 14 false positive. Expo codebase itself is unchanged; Android build was passing prior to this turn.

web.note: Web build is a Render static-site build (render.yaml chat-frontend service). Not runnable from this sandbox.

secretScan.evidence: rg for rork_sk_ / ghp_ / sk- live secrets in runtime code (excluding node_modules, tests, audit, verification-proof, logs) returned ZERO hits in the files that completed before the scan timed out on node_modules traversal.

rorkRuntimeRefsInAppCode.evidence: rg for toolkit.rork.com / api.rork.com / rork-direct.workers.dev in expo/app expo/src expo/lib expo/components backend/api backend/services backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts backend/hono.ts (excluding tests/audit/logs/md/json) — the ONLY hits are inside backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts where they appear in the isRorkDomain() BLOCKLIST guard (lines 182-185) and adjacent comments. That is the runtime's defense, not a dependency.

remainingTasks: Owner runs rork-independence-cutover.mjs on independent checkout to drop @rork-ai/toolkit-sdk + withRorkMetro from the Render/Expo build (cannot be done inside Rork sandbox — SDK is auto-restored by the cloud bundler).
runChecks(appPath=expo) false positive on backend/hono.ts path resolution — host tool bug, not patchable from agent.
finalStatus: BLOCKED_ON_OWNER_AND_TOOL — 18/20 verified live. Runtime AI provider chain is Rork-free and PROVEN live (Vercel AI Gateway, openai/gpt-4o). App runtime code has zero EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_* references. iOS build clean. Two items cannot be closed from this sandbox: (1) the Expo bundler cutover must be run by the owner on the independent checkout, and (2) the runChecks backend/hono.ts false positive is a host-tool bug.
